message:
Improve coordinator state management

- Enhance coordinator command handling
- Improve state management and persistence
- Add state tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"caam-lr2","title":"caam add: One-command account capture","description":"## Problem
Adding a new account is multi-step:
1. Clear existing auth (or login overwrites)
2. Run tool's login command
3. Complete OAuth flow
4. Run caam backup with a name

## Solution
One command that does everything:

```bash
caam add claude
# Current Claude auth will be cleared.
# You'll be prompted to login to a new account.
# Continue? [Y/n]: y

# Clearing existing Claude auth...
# Opening Claude login...
# [Browser opens, user completes OAuth]

# Login complete! Save as profile.
# Profile name: work-2
# ✓ Saved claude/work-2

# Activate this profile now? [Y/n]: y
# ✓ Activated claude/work-2
```

## Features
- Backs up current auth first (if exists and not already saved)
- Clears auth files
- Launches tool's login flow
- Waits for auth files to appear
- Prompts for profile name
- Backs up new auth
- Optionally activates

## Safety
- Auto-backup before clear (to _auto_backup_<timestamp>)
- Timeout if login doesn't complete
- Restore original on cancel

## Variations
```bash
caam add claude                    # Interactive
caam add claude my-profile         # Pre-specify name
caam add claude --no-activate      # Don't activate after
caam add --all                     # Add account for all tools
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-20T00:01:21.166053696Z","updated_at":"2025-12-20T02:29:03.380213603Z","closed_at":"2025-12-20T02:29:03.380213603Z","close_reason":"Implemented caam add command with tests - all 28 packages pass","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-lr2","depends_on_id":"caam-m9g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-20T00:03:38.557969289Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-ls9g","title":"Create internal/pricing package with provider pricing tables","description":"# Task: Create internal/pricing package with provider pricing tables

## Overview
Create a pricing package that contains up-to-date token pricing tables for all AI providers, enabling cost calculations and subscription value analysis.

## Background
Pay-per-token API pricing (as of 2025):

### Claude API
- claude-3-opus: $15/M input, $75/M output
- claude-3.5-sonnet: $3/M input, $15/M output
- claude-3-haiku: $0.25/M input, $1.25/M output
- claude-4-opus: TBD
- Cache read: 90% discount on input
- Cache creation: 25% premium on input

### OpenAI
- gpt-4o: $5/M input, $15/M output
- gpt-4o-mini: $0.15/M input, $0.60/M output

### Gemini
- gemini-ultra: TBD
- gemini-pro: TBD

## Technical Details

### Package Structure
```go
// internal/pricing/pricing.go

type TokenPrice struct {
    InputPer1M       float64
    OutputPer1M      float64
    CacheReadPer1M   float64  // Usually 10% of input
    CacheCreatePer1M float64  // Usually 125% of input
}

var ClaudePricing = map[string]TokenPrice{
    "claude-3-opus":      {15.00, 75.00, 1.50, 18.75},
    "claude-3.5-sonnet":  {3.00, 15.00, 0.30, 3.75},
    "claude-3-haiku":     {0.25, 1.25, 0.025, 0.3125},
}

// CalculateCost computes the total cost for token usage
func CalculateCost(usage *logs.TokenUsage, model, provider string) float64

// NormalizeModelName handles model name variations
func NormalizeModelName(model string) string
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Contains pricing for all major models
- [ ] Handles cache read/create token pricing
- [ ] Normalizes model name variants
- [ ] Returns accurate cost calculations
- [ ] Easy to update when pricing changes

## Files to Create
- internal/pricing/pricing.go
- internal/pricing/claude.go
- internal/pricing/openai.go
- internal/pricing/gemini.go
- internal/pricing/pricing_test.go

 {"id":"caam-m5a","title":"Display QR code for device auth URL (nice-to-have)","description":"# Display QR Code for Device Auth URL

## What

When using device code flow, display a QR code that users can scan with their phone to quickly reach the authorization URL.

## Why

QR codes provide faster mobile access:
- No typing long URLs on phones
- Works even when URL can't be copy-pasted (some SSH clients)
- Professional/polished UX
- Many CLI tools (gh, az) do this successfully

## Implementation

### Dependencies

Use a pure-Go QR code library to avoid external dependencies:
- github.com/mdp/qrterminal (MIT license, popular)
- github.com/skip2/go-qrcode (generates image, not terminal)

### File: internal/auth/qrcode.go

\`\`\`go
package auth

import (
    "io"
    "os"
    
    "github.com/mdp/qrterminal/v3"
    "golang.org/x/term"
)

// DisplayQRCode prints a QR code to the terminal if stdout is a TTY
func DisplayQRCode(url string, w io.Writer) {
    // Only show QR if we have a TTY (not piped/redirected)
    if !term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdout.Fd())) {
        return
    }
    
    // Use ASCII mode for compatibility
    config := qrterminal.Config{
        Level:     qrterminal.L,
        Writer:    w,
        BlackChar: qrterminal.WHITE, // Inverted for dark terminals
        WhiteChar: qrterminal.BLACK,
        QuietZone: 1,
    }
    qrterminal.GenerateWithConfig(url, config)
}
\`\`\`

### Integration in device code flow

\`\`\`go
func (p *Provider) loginWithDeviceCode(ctx context.Context, prof *profile.Profile) error {
    fmt.Println("Starting device code authentication...")
    fmt.Println("")
    
    // For Codex, we need to capture the output to get the code
    // Then display our enhanced version
    
    // Display QR code pointing to the auth URL
    authURL := "https://auth.openai.com/codex/device"
    auth.DisplayQRCode(authURL, os.Stdout)
    
    fmt.Println("")
    fmt.Printf("Open: %s\\n", authURL)
    fmt.Println("Or scan the QR code above with your phone")
    fmt.Println("")
    
    // Run the actual codex login --device-auth
    cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "codex", "login", "--device-auth")
    // ...
}
\`\`\`

## Output Example

\`\`\`
Starting device code authentication...

  █▀▀▀▀▀█ ▀▀▀▀█▀ █▀▀▀▀▀█
  █ ███ █ █▀▄█ ▀ █ ███ █
  █ ▀▀▀ █ ▀▄▀ ▄█ █ ▀▀▀ █
  ▀▀▀▀▀▀▀ ▀ █ ▀ ▀▀▀▀▀▀▀
  ██▀▀▀▀▀ ▀█▄ ▄▀▄▀█▄▀▄██
  ...

Open: https://auth.openai.com/codex/device
Or scan the QR code above with your phone

Enter code: ABCD-EFGH
\`\`\`

## UX Considerations

1. **Terminal compatibility**: QR codes may not render well in all terminals
   - Detect terminal capabilities if possible
   - Provide --no-qr flag to disable

2. **Color inversion**: Dark vs light terminals need different rendering
   - Try to auto-detect (COLORFGBG env var)
   - Provide --qr-invert flag

3. **Size**: QR code shouldn't overwhelm the terminal
   - Keep it reasonably sized
   - Use minimal quiet zone

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] QR code displays for device auth URL
- [ ] Works in common terminals (iTerm2, Terminal.app, GNOME Terminal)
- [ ] Gracefully skipped if not a TTY
- [ ] --no-qr flag to disable
- [ ] URL still printed as text (accessibility)

## Testing

- Visual test in various terminals
- Test with screen reader (ensure URL is still readable)
- Test when stdout is piped (QR should not appear)

## Notes

This is a polish/UX enhancement. Core device code flow should work without this.
Consider deferring until after core device code is working.

## Dependencies

- caam-ela: Codex device code implementation
- caam-v89: CLI --device-code flag","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-17T22:39:18.382441283Z","updated_at":"2025-12-17T23:06:45.861881769Z","closed_at":"2025-12-17T23:06:45.861881769Z","close_reason":"Removed as gold-plating. Codex already displays the URL clearly. QR code adds dependency and complexity for marginal UX gain. Keep caam simple.","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-m5a","depends_on_id":"caam-ela","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T22:40:18.087310678Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-m5a","depends_on_id":"caam-v89","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T22:40:23.188174111Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-m7dr","title":"Unit tests for refresh package (token management)","description":"# Refresh Package Unit Tests

## Current State

## Test Requirements

### 1. RefreshProfile Tests
- RefreshProfile() success path
- Provider-specific refresh logic
- Error handling and recovery
- Health storage updates

### 2. Claude Refresh Tests
- OAuth token refresh flow
- API key validation
- Token expiry detection
- HTTP mock for refresh endpoint

### 3. Codex Refresh Tests
- OAuth token refresh
- Session token handling
- Expiry calculation

### 4. Gemini Refresh Tests
- ADC token refresh
- gcloud credential handling
- API key mode (no refresh needed)

### 5. URL Guard Tests
- ValidateRefreshEndpoint()
- HTTPS enforcement
- Domain allowlist checking
- Malicious URL rejection

### 6. Rate Limit Detection Tests
- IsRateLimited() from response
- Error pattern matching
- Retry-After header parsing

## Implementation Notes
- Use httptest.Server for OAuth mocks
- Test both success and error responses
- Verify health updates after refresh

## Files to Modify

- internal/refresh/claude_test.go (expand)
- internal/refresh/codex_test.go (expand)
- internal/refresh/gemini_test.go (expand)
- internal/refresh/url_guard_test.go (expand)","notes":"Refresh package tests expanded to 67.8% coverage. Individual functions well-tested (many at 100%): ShouldRefresh, getRefreshTokenFromJSON, readStringField, readNestedStringField, validateTokenEndpoint, ReadADC, UpdateClaudeAuth (100%), UpdateCodexAuth (100%). RefreshClaudeToken/Codex/Gemini at 85%+. Orchestration functions (RefreshProfile, refresh*) need E2E testing as they require full vault/health/HTTP integration.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-21T17:03:26.189250064Z","updated_at":"2026-01-10T22:14:42.988634925Z","closed_at":"2026-01-10T22:14:42.988634925Z","close_reason":"Achieved 80.6% coverage for refresh package (target was 80%).  {"id":"caam-m9g","title":"EPIC: Just Works UX - Zero-friction account switching","description":"## Vision
Transform CAAM from "useful CLI tool" to "invisible magic that just works."

## Success Criteria
- New user productive in <2 minutes (vs current 10+ minutes)
- Daily use requires zero caam commands (shell wrapper handles everything)
- Rate limit recovery is automatic (detect, cooldown, switch, retry)
- Profile management is intuitive (aliases, workspaces, fuzzy matching)

## Core Features (Priority Order)

### P0 - Foundation (Must complete first)
- [x] **caam-q9f**: Wrapper Mode - `caam run <tool>` with auto rate-limit detection and retry
- [x] **caam-bxq**: Init Wizard - Guided setup with auth discovery

### P1 - Shell Integration (Enables invisible UX)
- [ ] **caam-k9z**: Shell integration - aliases, completion, prompt, auto-activate
- [ ] **caam-hxk**: `caam next` - One-command profile rotation

### P2 - Power Features
- [ ] **caam-08w**: Profile aliases and fuzzy matching
- [ ] **caam-t5p**: Profile workspaces - Switch all tools at once
- [ ] **caam-lr2**: `caam add` - One-command account capture
- [ ] **caam-ah4**: Auth file watcher with auto-backup

### P3 - Polish
- [ ] **caam-vog**: `caam verify` - Validate all profile tokens
- [ ] **caam-0l0**: Background daemon for auto-refresh and sync

## Dependency Graph
```
                    ┌─────────────────┐
                    │   caam-m9g      │
                    │  (this epic)    │
                    └────────┬────────┘
                             │
        ┌────────────────────┼────────────────────┐
        │                    │                    │
        ▼                    ▼                    ▼
   ┌─────────┐         ┌─────────┐         ┌─────────┐
   │caam-q9f │         │caam-bxq │         │caam-hxk │
   │ wrapper │         │  init   │         │  next   │
   └────┬────┘         └─────────┘         └─────────┘
        │
        ▼
   ┌─────────┐
   │caam-k9z │ (shell integration uses wrapper)
   │  shell  │
   └─────────┘
```

## Design Principles
1. **Invisible by default** - Works without thinking about it
2. **Progressive disclosure** - Simple commands, advanced options available
3. **Safe by default** - Auto-backup, no data loss
4. **Fast feedback** - Clear status, quick operations

## Anti-patterns to Avoid
- Requiring users to understand internal architecture
- Multi-step workflows for common operations
- Silent failures or unclear error messages
- Configuration complexity

## What "Just Works" Means

**Before (current state):**
```bash
# User hits rate limit in Claude
# Manually notices the error
caam cooldown set claude/work
caam ls claude  # remember what profiles exist
caam activate claude personal
claude "retry my prompt"
```

**After (with this epic complete):**
```bash
claude "do something"
# Rate limit happens automatically handled
# User sees: "⚠️ Rate limit. Switching to 'personal'..."
# Command continues with new profile
```

## Implementation Order
1. **caam-q9f** (Wrapper Mode) - Core capability
2. **caam-bxq** (Init Wizard) - Onboarding experience
3. **caam-k9z** (Shell Integration) - Makes wrapper invisible
4. **caam-hxk** (caam next) - Quick manual switching
5. P2/P3 features in any order

## Measuring Success
- Time to first profile saved: target <2 minutes
- Daily caam commands required: target 0 (shell wrapper handles all)
- Rate limit recovery time: target <5 seconds (automatic)
- User confusion incidents: target 0 (intuitive UX)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-20T00:02:55.725871327Z","updated_at":"2025-12-20T02:50:19.217733098Z","closed_at":"2025-12-20T02:50:19.217733098Z","close_reason":"All sub-items complete: P0 (wrapper, init), P1 (shell, next), P2 (aliases, workspaces, add, authwatch), P3 (verify, daemon). Zero-friction account switching achieved.","compaction_level":0}

 {"id":"caam-v89","title":"Add --device-code flag to caam login command","description":"# Add --device-code flag to caam login (Profile Isolation Mode)

## IMPORTANT: Primary Workflow Doesn't Need This

For auth file swapping (the primary use case), users can already do:
\`\`\`bash
codex login --device-auth    # Native Codex command
caam backup codex account1   # Existing caam command
\`\`\`

No changes to caam needed for the primary workflow!

## When This Is Useful

This flag is for **Profile Isolation mode** - when users want isolated CODEX_HOME directories for parallel sessions:

\`\`\`bash
# Create isolated profiles with device-code auth
caam login codex work --device-code     # Isolated profile "work"
caam login codex personal --device-code # Isolated profile "personal"

# Run parallel sessions
caam exec codex work -- "task A"
caam exec codex personal -- "task B"   # Different account, simultaneous
\`\`\`

## Implementation (Simple)

Just pass \`--device-auth\` to the underlying \`codex login\` when flag is set:

\`\`\`go
if loginDeviceCode {
    if tool == "codex" {
        args = append(args, "--device-auth")
    } else {
        return fmt.Errorf("%s doesn't support --device-code\\n\\n"+
            "For headless setup, use native login + caam backup:\\n"+
            "  %s login\\n"+
            "  caam backup %s <name>", tool, tool, tool)
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Priority: P2

Useful for advanced Profile Isolation users, but primary workflow already works.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-17T22:38:13.433808220Z","updated_at":"2025-12-18T01:51:42.676486082Z","closed_at":"2025-12-18T01:51:42.676486082Z","close_reason":"Added --device-code flag to caam login; uses provider DeviceCodeProvider to run device-auth flow for codex isolated profiles.","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-v89","depends_on_id":"caam-hzu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T22:40:07.882564117Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":28,"issue_id":"caam-v89","author":"ubuntu","text":"Starting: add --device-code to `caam login` (isolated profiles) and route to provider.DeviceCodeProvider when supported (Codex => codex login --device-auth). Will add tests + update codex provider SupportedAuthModes.","created_at":"2025-12-18T01:48:29Z"},{"id":30,"issue_id":"caam-v89","author":"ubuntu","text":"Landed in ff48b6d: `caam login codex <profile> --device-code` calls DeviceCodeProvider.LoginWithDeviceCode; errors if provider does not support device code. Tests updated for login flag presence.","created_at":"2025-12-18T01:51:52Z"}]}
 {"id":"caam-vek1","title":"Implement JWT parser for Codex auth.json","description":"# Task: Implement JWT parser for Codex auth.json

## Overview
Create a JWT parser that extracts identity information (email, organization, plan type) from the JWT ID token stored in Codex auth.json files WITHOUT making API calls.

## Background
OAuth tokens contain a JWT ID token with three parts: header.payload.signature (base64 encoded). The payload contains "claims" including:
- email: The account email
- plan_type or subscription_type: "max", "pro", "team", "enterprise"
- org or organization: Organization name (team accounts)
- exp: Expiration timestamp

We can base64-decode the payload WITHOUT verifying the signature (we trust it since it came from the provider).

## Technical Details

### Package: internal/identity/

```go
type Identity struct {
    Email        string    `json:"email,omitempty"`
    Organization string    `json:"organization,omitempty"`
    PlanType     string    `json:"plan_type,omitempty"`
    AccountID    string    `json:"account_id,omitempty"`
    ExpiresAt    time.Time `json:"expires_at,omitempty"`
    Provider     string    `json:"provider"`
}

// ExtractFromJWT parses a JWT token and extracts identity claims
func ExtractFromJWT(token string) (*Identity, error) {
    // 1. Split token by "."
    // 2. Base64-decode the payload (middle part)
    // 3. JSON unmarshal into claims struct
    // 4. Map claims to Identity struct
}

// ExtractFromCodexAuth reads Codex auth.json and extracts identity
func ExtractFromCodexAuth(path string) (*Identity, error) {
    // 1. Read auth.json
    // 2. Find id_token field
    // 3. Call ExtractFromJWT
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Correctly parses valid JWT tokens
- [ ] Extracts email from 100% of valid tokens
- [ ] Extracts plan_type when present
- [ ] Handles malformed tokens gracefully (returns error, not panic)
- [ ] Sub-millisecond extraction time (no network calls)
- [ ] Works offline

## Test Cases
1. Parse valid JWT with all fields
2. Parse JWT with minimal fields (email only)
3. Handle expired JWT (still extract identity)
4. Handle malformed JWT (invalid base64)
5. Handle JWT with unknown claims (ignore extra fields)

## Files to Create
- internal/identity/identity.go
- internal/identity/jwt.go
- internal/identity/codex.go
- internal/identity/identity_test.go

 {"id":"caam-vog","title":"caam verify: Validate all profile tokens","description":"## Problem
Profiles may have expired tokens or invalid auth without user knowing.

## Solution
Verification command that tests all profiles.

```bash
caam verify

Checking profile health...

Claude:
  ✓ work        Token valid (expires in 23h)
  ✓ personal    Token valid (expires in 4d)
  ✗ old-backup  Token expired (2 weeks ago)

Codex:
  ✓ dev         Token valid (expires in 6h) 
  ⚠ staging     Token expiring soon (45 min)

Gemini:
  ✓ main        Token valid (expires in 12h)

Summary: 5 healthy, 1 expiring soon, 1 expired

Recommendations:
  - Run 'caam refresh codex staging' to refresh expiring token
  - Delete or re-login to 'claude/old-backup'
```

## Features
1. Check token expiry for all profiles
2. Optionally verify with API call (--deep)
3. Show actionable recommendations
4. Return non-zero if issues found (for CI)

## Flags
```bash
caam verify              # Quick check (parse files only)
caam verify --deep       # API validation (slower)
caam verify --fix        # Auto-refresh expiring tokens
caam verify --json       # Machine-readable output
caam verify claude       # Check specific tool only
```

## Integration
- Could run automatically in `caam status`
- Daemon could run periodically
- CI/CD could verify before deployment","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-20T00:02:33.203251120Z","updated_at":"2025-12-20T02:49:10.846649627Z","closed_at":"2025-12-20T02:49:10.846649627Z","close_reason":"Implemented caam verify command with --json support, health calculation, and recommendations. 8 tests included.","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-vog","depends_on_id":"caam-m9g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-20T00:03:58.987006271Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"caam-vpu2","title":"Feature: WezTerm recover command (state machine)","description":"Goal
- Automate the full per-pane recovery flow after rate limit: /login -> select subscription -> detect URL -> wait for code -> inject code -> resume prompt.

State Machine (per pane)
- IDLE: normal output
- RATE_LIMITED: detect rate-limit banner
- LOGIN_SENT: inject /login
- METHOD_SELECT: detect login method prompt -> inject 1
- URL_READY: extract OAuth URL
- CODE_WAIT: prompt operator / await local agent response
- CODE_SENT: inject code + Enter
- RESUME: detect login success -> inject resume prompt

UX + Safety
- Show a table of panes with state, match reason, and URL availability.
- Default to manual confirmation; never act on non-matching panes unless --all is explicit.
- Per-pane cooldown to avoid repeated injections when output is noisy.
- Manual override per pane (skip/force/retry).

Tests
- Unit tests for state machine transitions (see caam-tc6p).
- E2E tests with mocked wezterm CLI (see caam-6sao.2).
- Structured logs with pane_id, state, action, and errors.

Success
- Operator can recover 5-10 panes in one loop with minimal typing and clear feedback.

+{"id":"caam-vpu2","title":"Feature: WezTerm recover command (state machine)","description":"Goal
- Automate the full per-pane recovery flow after rate limit: /login -> select subscription -> detect URL -> wait for code -> inject code -> resume prompt.

State Machine (per pane)
- IDLE: normal output
- RATE_LIMITED: detect rate-limit banner
- LOGIN_SENT: inject /login
- METHOD_SELECT: detect login method prompt -> inject 1
- URL_READY: extract OAuth URL
- CODE_WAIT: prompt operator / await local agent response
- CODE_SENT: inject code + Enter
- RESUME: detect login success -> inject resume prompt

UX + Safety
- Show a table of panes with state, match reason, and URL availability.
- Default to manual confirmation; never act on non-matching panes unless --all is explicit.
- Per-pane cooldown to avoid repeated injections when output is noisy.
- Manual override per pane (skip/force/retry).

Tests
- Unit tests for state machine transitions (see caam-tc6p).
- E2E tests with mocked wezterm CLI (see caam-6sao.2).
- Structured logs with pane_id, state, action, and errors.

Success
- Operator can recover 5-10 panes in one loop with minimal typing and clear feedback.

 {"id":"caam-vxpx","title":"Task: Compaction reminder injection logic","description":"Goal
- Inject reminder prompt once per compaction event per pane.

Details
- Per-pane cooldown (configurable, default 5-10 minutes).
- Skip if reminder already appears in recent output.
- Use wezterm send-text with --no-paste.
- Guard against repeated detection loops.

Success
- No spam in real sessions; correct injection after compaction.","notes":"Depends on: caam-wgd8 (detection regex) and caam-egeq (config toggles).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:18:52.137583586Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T17:19:29.072704866Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-vxpx","depends_on_id":"caam-egeq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:56:02.970218526Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-vxpx","depends_on_id":"caam-imtg","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:18:52.138892418Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-vxpx","depends_on_id":"caam-imtg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:19:15.822312995Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-vxpx","depends_on_id":"caam-wgd8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:55:58.376778513Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-w5op","title":"CLI: Add warning when all profiles for provider are in cooldown","description":"Show warning in status when ALL profiles for a provider are in cooldown.

SCENARIO: User has 3 Claude profiles, all hit rate limits. Status should warn:
  claude: ⚠️ ALL profiles in cooldown (next available in 23m)

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. After processing each provider, check if any profile is NOT in cooldown
2. If all are in cooldown:
   - Find the one with shortest remaining cooldown
   - Show warning with time until next available
3. Add to warnings list for display at end

This is critical UX - user needs to know they can't use ANY profile for this provider.

FILES: cmd/caam/cmd/root.go","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-20T01:26:01.525844528Z","updated_at":"2025-12-20T01:52:00.667597763Z","closed_at":"2025-12-20T01:52:00.667597763Z","close_reason":"Implemented all-profiles cooldown warning: shows when all profiles for a provider are in cooldown with next-available time","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-w5op","depends_on_id":"caam-gy7z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-20T01:27:27.607528387Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
